sections.paragraph: fem. barley, so called from its hairy ears, like the Lat. hordeum a horrendo, and on the contrary בֻּסֶּמֶת spelt (which see), from its shorn ears. In sing. it is used of barley growing, Job 31:40 Joe 1:11 but pl. שְׂעֹרִים of the grain (see חִטָּה ). חֹמֶר שְׂעֹרִים a homer of barley, Lev 27:16, אֵיפַת שְׂעֹרִים an ephah of barley, Rth 2:17 also קְצִיר שְׂעֹרִים barley harvest, Rth 1:22, since the harvest is on account of the grain.
